digest.top_line: PRIMARY2: PSMA-PET avoided biopsy in 49% of men while staying non-inferior for csPCa detection (12% vs 16%) vs systematic biopsy in high-risk equivocal-MRI men.
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day's only signal, and it's diagnostic, not therapeutic. PRIMARY2, a phase 3 RCT, backs the PSMA-PET PRIMARY-score pathway as a biopsy-avoidance gate, sparing biopsy in ~49% of biopsy-naive high-risk men with equivocal MRI while holding non-inferior csPCa detection. Wide 10% NI margin and immature follow-up temper the read.

sites.intro: Diagnostic-pathway data: PSMA-PET as a biopsy-avoidance gate in biopsy-naive high-risk men with equivocal MRI.

studies.name: PRIMARY2
studies.tldr: PSMA-PET avoided biopsy in 49% while staying non-inferior for csPCa detection (12% vs 16%) vs systematic biopsy in high-risk equivocal-MRI men.
details: 🔍 Phase 3 non-inferiority RCT, 7 Australian sites, N=660, randomised 1:1 PSMA-PET-CT vs systematic transperineal biopsy, no masking
🔍 Biopsy-naive, high clinical risk, PI-RADS 2 (51%) or 3 (49%) MRI, PSA ≤20 (median 5·2), ≤cT2
🔍 PRIMARY score 3-5 on PSMA-PET → targeted biopsy; score 1-2 → biopsy avoided

details.text: 📊 Co-primary endpoints, PSMA-PET vs systematic biopsy

details: 🔗 Phase 3 confirmation of the PRIMARY (Emmett et al) PSMA-PET PRIMARY-score pathway for csPCa detection
⚠️ Non-inferiority design with a wide 10% margin; csPCa detection point estimate favoured control, raising missed-cancer vs avoided-overdiagnosis question
⚠️ Follow-up ongoing; whether biopsy-avoided men later develop csPCa is not yet established
⚠️ Open-label, single-country, Ga-PSMA-11 only; generalisability to F-18 PSMA tracers and other settings unproven

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 non-inferiority RCT, co-primary endpoints met, contests biopsy-all pathway. Follow-up ongoing, 10% NI margin wide, Ga-PSMA-11-specific, single-country; short of practice-changing.
verdict.audience: Biopsy-naive, high clinical risk, PI-RADS 2-3 MRI, PSA ≤20 ng/mL

open_questions: Do biopsy-avoided men later develop clinically significant prostate cancer?
Generalisability to other PSMA radiopharmaceuticals (F-18 tracers) and non-Australian settings

related_trials.brief_title: Evaluation of Prostate Specific Membrane Antigen Positron Emission Tomography-Computed Tomography in Active Surveillance for Prostate CancEr
related_trials.brief_summary: This study will be assessing the ability of PSMA-PET CT to determine the absence of clinically significant prostate cancer in patients on active surveillance (AS) with low risk and favorable intermediate-risk prostate cancer.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Males aged ≥ 18.
* Histologically confirmed low or favorable intermediate risk prostate cancer per NCCN guidelines. (Note: Grade Group 2 must have 20% or less involvement in every core and no presence of cribiform or intraductal carcinoma).
* PSA \< 20 ng/ml.
* Ability to…
related_trials.answers_question: Do biopsy-avoided men later develop clinically significant prostate cancer?
related_trials.relevance_phrase: PSMA-PET to rule out csPCa in active surveillance

related_trials.brief_summary: The goal of this study to determine if using the (18)F-rhPSMA-7.3 PET-CT scan can improve the ability to spot serious prostate cancer in patients who have uncertain MRI results. These uncertain results include MRI results that showed a PIRADS 2 or PIRADS 3 area on the prostate. The investigators want to find out if this new test is better to detect prostate cancer than just doing a biopsy based on MRI alone.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. The patient is willing to provide signed informed consent and is able to comply with all required study-related procedures, where safe and feasible.
2. The patient is male and at least 18 years of age.
3. The patient has not previously undergone a prostate biopsy.

related_trials.brief_title: Comparing a New PSMA Imaging Agent to MRI for Detecting Prostate Cancer, BEACON Trial
related_trials.brief_summary: This early phase I trial evaluates whether a new imaging technique using flotufolastat F 18 (a type of prostate specific membrane antigen \[PSMA\] imaging agent) with positron emission tomography (PET)/computed tomography (CT) can be used to guide targeted prostate biopsies in men with prostate cancer. Flotufolastat F 18 is a radioactive imaging agent that binds to prostate tumor cells that express PSMA. This allows for visualization of PSMA-expressing tumor cells on imaging scans such as…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

* Men aged 18-90 at study enrollment
* Have at least one PI-RADS 3-5 lesion on MRI within the 12 months prior to enrollment

papers.title: Effect of [68Ga]Ga-PSMA-11 PET-CT in the diagnosis of prostate cancer in men with equivocal or clinically high-risk non-suspicious findings on multiparametric MRI (PRIMARY2): a multicentre, non-inferiority, phase 3, randomised controlled trial
